canonical_title: OpenAI GPT-5.6 broad public rollout after U.S. government review
aliases: GPT-5.6 Sol Terra Luna public launch
treatment: Treat as a model availability and policy-governance topic. Reinclude only if OpenAI publishes final availability/pricing/API docs, the public rollout is delayed or restricted again, Commerce/White House issues a binding rule or correction, third-party evals reveal material capability/safety results, or GPT-5.6 Sol/Terra/Luna materially change coding, bio, cybersecurity, agentic, cost, or access assumptions.
known_facts: Reuters reported on 2026-07-08 that OpenAI will publicly launch GPT-5.6 on Thursday after a June delay requested by the U.S. government over national-security concerns.
Axios reported that a broad launch followed additional testing and meetings between OpenAI and government officials, while a White House official said no approval or clearance is legally required.
The family includes GPT-5.6 Sol as the flagship model and lower-cost Terra and Luna variants.
Reuters and Business Insider reported that OpenAI positioned Sol around improved agentic capabilities in coding, biology, and cybersecurity.
The story is part of a broader pattern where frontier-model availability is being negotiated through voluntary government testing and release governance rather than purely product-led rollout.
reinclude_only_if: OpenAI publishes official release notes, model cards, API pricing, availability tiers, or ChatGPT plan access details for GPT-5.6 Sol/Terra/Luna.
The public rollout is delayed, region-restricted, export-restricted, or otherwise materially changed after this report.
U.S. Department of Commerce, White House, or other government body issues a binding rule, denial, correction, or enforcement action about GPT-5.6 or frontier model pre-release testing.
Credible third-party benchmarks or safety evaluations show material capability or risk changes in coding, biology, cybersecurity, agentic workflows, or jailbreak robustness.
OpenAI confirms or changes safeguards, dangerous capability thresholds, or government-review workflow for future frontier models.
notes: Adopted as the 2026-07-08 Night Lite delta after the midday Joshua Achiam departure item. This is a separate OpenAI model availability and frontier-model governance story, not a personnel-governance repeat.
